MERIT Documentation¶

This documentation is the implementation-level reference for MERIT (MachinE learning ReadIness for Tabular metabolomics data).

Scope¶

This guide is intentionally detailed and code-aligned. It covers:

  • End-to-end pipeline and data model

  • Every scoring section and every metric currently implemented

  • Exact formulas, thresholds, and section aggregation behavior

  • Gate logic (G1-G5), provisional vs final banding, and gate ceilings

  • UI output semantics, including examples of descriptor/tooltip text

  • Practical usage for CLI, UI, and reproducible assessment runs

Read this in order if you are new to MERIT.

Core Guide

  • Quickstart
    • Prerequisites
    • Run one study via CLI (ingest -> normalize -> assess -> report)
    • Build this documentation locally
    • ReadTheDocs setup
  • Pipeline and Data Model
    • Pipeline overview
    • Multi-source behavior
    • Source-aware zero handling
    • Canonical data model
    • FeatureMatrix
    • Assessment report object
  • Scoring Framework and Gates
    • High-level design
    • Section score computation
    • Current fixed section counts
    • Core and reusability aggregation
    • Band thresholds
    • Feasibility gates (G1-G5)
    • Gate ceiling logic
    • Why annotation is in core score
    • Informational metrics
  • Output Schema and Interpretation
    • Assessment report file
    • Metric result object
    • Readiness score payload
    • Meaning of key fields
    • informational metrics
  • UI Text and Tooltip Reference
    • Purpose
    • Overview panel text
    • Gate labels shown in overview
    • Analytical QC text examples
    • Outlier burden explanatory text includes
    • ML Feasibility tooltips
    • Cohort/Bias tooltip examples
    • Informational-only badge behavior
    • Maintaining sync between docs and UI strings
  • FAQ
    • What does MERIT score: trainability only or interpretability too?
    • Why is Metadata / FAIR reported separately?
    • Why can final band be lower than provisional band?
    • Why can an informational metric show WARN?
    • Which metrics are informational today?
    • How does source-aware missingness work?
    • Are QC/blank/pool samples excluded from scoring?
    • How should I interpret class separability?
    • How do I keep docs accurate after metric changes?
